audit_history.scope: NMR Code Fix Audit (multisig wallet security fixes)
NMR 2.0 Contract Upgrade (supply reduction, immutability enablement)

upgrade_2019.changes: Reduced max supply to 11 million; disabled minting, multisig, emergency stop, upgradeability; enabled burning only
upgrade_2019.rationale: Move towards decentralization (Richard Craib); put entire supply in play; give total control to smart contract
upgrade_2019.security_improvement: Converted from upgradeable to immutable contract for supply cap enforcement

upgrade_mechanism.details: 11 million token cap is now permanently enforced by immutable contract code. No future upgrades possible. Minting disabled; only burning mechanism remains (repurposed from original mint function).
upgrade_mechanism.governance: No governance needed for contract changes — supply cap is cryptographic law

security_features.approve_mechanism: Custom changeApproval() function required for non-zero to non-zero allowance changes
security_features.custody_features: Staking and burning; stake burning on poor prediction performance

treasury_security.signers: Numerai team (number of signers/threshold not publicly disclosed)
treasury_security.details: Centralized multisig for governance; no public disclosure of signer count or threshold

bug_bounty.bounty_mechanism: Ad-hoc bounties for high-impact findings (e.g., ability to steal NMR = full stake value)
bug_bounty.notes: Small project with niche audience; no Immunefi listing found. Bounties available via direct disclosure.

2018_multisig_issue.description: Minor security vulnerabilities in multisignature wallet
2018_multisig_issue.resolution: Temporary patch implemented; completely replaced with Gnosis Safe in NMR 2.0

security_assessment.notes: Mature, simple ERC-20 with minimal dependencies. Immutable post-2019. No major exploits on record. Audit trail is well-documented in GitHub repository.

metadata.sources: Free public sources (GitHub, Etherscan, Web search, official docs)
metadata.completeness: Complete audit trail; immutability confirmed; no known exploits
